summary: Martin Fowler shares his experience with the Google Nexus 7 tablet after several months of use. He purchased it to replace an aging iPad and to experiment with both Android and the smaller 7-inch form factor. While he finds Google's base UI slightly superior to Apple's and appreciates Android's keyboard completion approach, he has mixed feelings about the smaller screen size. He concludes that despite using the 7-inch more at home, he would choose a 10-inch tablet if limited to one device due to the screen size requirements for certain content.
key_points: Android's UI has advantages over iOS, particularly the cross-application back button and word completion without automatic spacing
The 7-inch form factor is lighter and more convenient most of the time, but the 10-inch screen is essential for certain content like PDFs, code, and complex websites
If forced to choose one tablet size, the 10-inch wins despite the 7-inch being used more frequently at home
iOS still has an edge in application quality and availability, though this may be partly due to familiarity
money_quotes: I miss the cross-application back button when I go back to the iPad.
There are times - some websites, pdf documents, or books with code or graphics - when that bigger screen is essential.
If I could only have one tablet, it would have to be a 10â, but I do use the 7â more often at home.
I like to wait until I've used a device for a while before I post my experiences of it.
key_insight: When choosing between tablet sizes, occasional strong needs for a larger screen outweigh the frequent mild preference for smaller, lighter devices.
